entries: Added lib/publisher.js batch generation/publish/rollback lifecycle with audit trail, /api/publish/batch API (GET+POST), and full /admin/publish UI with draft preview, confirm publish, and rollback support.
Updated homepage data loading (lib/data.js) to prefer the latest published batch events over seed data, with seamless fallback to current-week.json.
Made public /api/events respect published batch visibility — only published batch events shown publicly, admin gets ?all=1 bypass.
Marked T-045 through T-049 (publishing system) done.

Built the approved-event asset pipeline: stage-1 approval now creates pending asset records, lib/assets.js orchestrates generation, and /api/assets/generate can generate or regenerate outputs for approved events.

Added sharp-based image processing with standardized portrait/square card framing, safe zones, text overlays, WebP/AVIF still outputs, and selected-image/fallback-image handling via lib/image-processor.js.

Connected local ffmpeg animation generation for simple Ken Burns MP4 outputs, added still-only fallback handling, surfaced generated previews plus regenerate controls in /admin/assets, and marked T-038 through T-044 done.
Verified production build/smoke-test path, synced stale public/data/tasks.json with the canonical tasks data, and redeployed okletsgo.ca to the current enrichment checkpoint.
Added source/ticket link capture, image-candidate gathering with provenance metadata, and category-tag heuristics across fetchers, normalization, import, and the JSON-backed db shape.
Surfaced source links, ticket links, tags, and image candidate counts in /admin/candidates and /admin/sources, and updated schema/docs for T-033 through T-036.

Added transparent confidence scoring, venue/address enrichment, and candidate import-to-review flow for source runs; /admin/sources now shows score + enrichment summaries and can import deduped candidates into /admin/candidates.
Extended the JSON-backed db shape with venue records plus candidate import dedupe checks, preserving scraper:<id> provenance while keeping the current app stable.

Added admin password auth with signed cookie login/logout, protected /admin plus admin mutation APIs, and wired source normalization/dedupe summaries; T-017, T-027, and T-028 are done while T-018 stays open with a production DB scaffold and documented blocker.
Added lib/db.js adapter boundary with FileDbAdapter, migrated app routes to use it, and documented future SQLite/Postgres path in docs/persistence.md
Built first source ingestion stack: lib/sources.js, fetcher registry, tourismkelowna + eventbrite-kelowna fetchers, /api/sources, /api/sources/run, and /admin/sources runner UI
Built manual candidate intake: /admin/manual plus /api/events/create with persistent candidate writes and admin nav updates
Tracked manual vs scraper source provenance in stored events and marked T-026, T-068, and T-069 done; left T-018 open pending a non-JSON backend behind the new adapter boundary
Converted site from static shell to real Next.js app with proper routes
Real /admin route with ops dashboard (week status, stats, nav)
Real /tasks route with numbered task checklist
SSH git auth fixed — pushes now work silently
okletsgo.ca domain correctly aliased to current deployment
Added manual event entry tasks (T-068, T-069) to pipeline plan
Wired double approval workflow — candidate and asset review screens now functional with persistent state
Wired review buttons, audit log, mobile UX polish, first source list defined

items.text: Stop extending the current static-shell workaround
Choose the real app rebuild path as canonical
Preserve current public visuals as the design target
Current week seed data file exists
Rebuild homepage as a real app route
Render cards from structured weekly data
Keep optimized local media pipeline working
Make current week fully driven by published batch data, not seed-only data
Create actual /admin route in a real app structure
Protect admin access properly without blocking public site
Replace ad hoc JSON with real persistent storage
Define schema for sources/raw events/normalized events/event occurrences/venues/image candidates/assets/reviews/publish batches
Implement state machine for event lifecycle
Add review actions: approve/reject/defer/reassign day/reassign mode/choose image/regenerate asset/hold
Record who approved what and when
Manual event entry form in admin (same pipeline as scraped events)
Track event source: manual vs scraper:<name> in schema
Connect it to approved event records instead of manual assets
Make homepage read latest published batch
Make unpublished drafts invisible to public
Support rollback to previous published batch
Store every week as a snapshot on publish
Inspect previous weeks with full event list
Define newsletter output from same approved batch
Set proper commit cadence in practice, not just intention
Remove dead admin fallback experiments once real app is in place
Document the final architecture as it changes
Port the current homepage into that app with identical visuals
Create a real /admin route shell in that same app
